title: Vision Sequences for Detecting Nine Targets
description:
Vision Sequences for Detecting Nine Targets

Vision Sequences for Detecting Nine Targets

Vision sequences necessary for calibration of fixed downward or standalone camera whose reference point type is "TaughtPoints (teach point)" must locate nine calibration targets.  There are two methods to do this.

	Create an object for each target, for a total
	 of nine objects.

OR

Use one of the multi-result objects (Blob
	 or Correlation objects) to return nine results. (See the section Working with
	 Multiple Results from a Single Object.)

Vision objects placed in order for Standalone Calibration

The objects must be placed on the image window in the same order that the targets will be taught.  The first object will be in the upper left corner, the second at top center, the third in the upper right corner, etc.

Note: For 'Fixed Downward' calibrations, the order for the middle row of objects is in reverse order: 6, 5, 4. This is because moving to the positions in the order 1, 2, 3, 6, 5,4, 7, 8, 9 produces the most efficient motion from the robot and helps make calibration complete faster.
